title: JSON Schema validation core and custom-keyword extension point
description:
The JSON Schema validation core: validate a projection against a rule's schema using the `jsonschema` crate, with cached schema compilation.

## Background

This is the engine's evaluation primitive and the fan-in point for shorthand desugaring and graph rules. It must compile each schema once, never per write. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` ("Validation flow") and decision record section 5.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] `jsonschema` (~0.46) is added as a dependency and configured for Draft 2020-12 explicitly.
- [ ] [hard] `validation/engine.rs` validates a `Projection` (as `serde_json::Value`) against a rule's JSON Schema and returns a `Finding { rule, severity, message }` per failure.
- [ ] [hard] Compiled schemas are cached (compiled once per rule, lazily); a unit test asserts no per-call recompilation.
- [ ] [hard] `contains`/`minContains`/`maxContains` validate as expected under the chosen draft, covered by a test using the `[hard]` criterion pattern.
- [ ] [hard] The core does NOT implement custom `x-jit-*` keywords (that is a separate task).

## Notes

`schemars` 0.8 is already present (used for projection schema generation). Cache via a `OnceCell`/`HashMap` on the engine; build the validator once per command invocation.
